questions.question: Which state has been declared the country’s first paperless judiciary state?
questions.explanation: Sikkim has been declared India’s first paperless judiciary state. The initiative aims to create a faster, transparent, and technology-driven judiciary. It emphasises the use of e-filing and digital case management systems. These digital tools are expected to improve efficiency and accessibility of justice delivery. The move will also make the judicial system more inclusive and user-friendly. The initiative aligns with India’s vision of Viksit Bharat 2047. A technology-driven judiciary will help ensure fairness and build public trust. It will also contribute to sustainable development by strengthening governance systems.

questions.question: Which state has launched India’s first digital grievance redressal system for gig workers?
questions.explanation: Karnataka has launched India’s first digital grievance redressal system for gig workers on May 1, 2026. The system allows platform-based workers to report disputes related to pay and working conditions. It has been developed by the Karnataka Platform-based Gig Workers Board and the e-Governance Department. The system enables real-time tracking of grievances filed by workers. It addresses issues such as wages, incentives, and working conditions. The initiative aims to ensure faster and more efficient dispute resolution. It seeks to bring transparency and accountability into the gig economy.

questions.question: The Chitra Pournami Festival is primarily celebrated in which state?
questions.explanation: The Chitra Pournami is primarily celebrated in Tamil Nadu with notable observances at the Mangaladevi Temple. The festival occurs on the full moon day in the Tamil month of Chithirai (April–May). In 2026, it was celebrated on 1 May. It is dedicated to Chitragupta and also associated with Goddess Mangaladevi (Kannagi). Large-scale celebrations take place in Thiruvannamalai, where devotees perform Girivalam around the sacred hill. The festival reflects spiritual purification, devotion, and the belief in karmic accountability.

questions.question: Ishank Singh, who has become the youngest swimmer to cross the Palk Strait, belongs to which state?
questions.explanation: A seven-year-old boy from Jharkhand has become the youngest swimmer to cross the Palk Strait. Ishank Singh completed the 29-kilometre stretch between Sri Lanka and India. He swam from Talaimannar to Dhanushkodi. He completed the swim in 9 hours and 50 minutes despite strong currents and rough seas. The achievement was recognised by the Universal Records Forum. The event was part of a campaign named “Mission Samandar”. His feat highlights exceptional endurance and determination at a very young age.

questions.question: Which state has launched the “Khuarel” mobile application to strengthen disaster response mechanisms?
questions.explanation: Mizoram has launched the “Khuarel” mobile application to strengthen disaster response mechanisms. The app is a real-time disaster incident reporting and management system. The application enables citizens to quickly report disaster-related incidents. It improves coordination and information flow during emergencies. The system enhances timely decision-making and disaster management effectiveness. Mizoram is among the first states in Northeast India to adopt such an advanced digital solution. The initiative reflects the growing use of technology in disaster preparedness and response.

questions.question: Who has won the 2026 Whitley Award for protecting the endangered Indian skimmer?
questions.explanation: Parveen Shaikh has won the 2026 Whitley Award for protecting the endangered Indian skimmer. She works with the Bombay Natural History Society. Her initiative “Guardians of the Skimmer” focuses on conservation along the Chambal River. The award, known as the “Green Oscars,” is given by the Whitley Fund for Nature in London. Her project has increased the skimmer population from about 400 in 2017 to over 1,000 by 2025. The programme uses local community members as “nest guardians” to protect nesting sites. It has significantly improved nest survival rates and reduced threats from predators and human disturbance.

questions.question: World Tuna Day is observed annually on which day?
questions.explanation: World Tuna Day is observed annually on May 2 to promote sustainable fishing and conservation of tuna species. The observance is supported by the United Nations along with governments and non-governmental organisations. In 2026, it is observed on Saturday, May 2. Nearly 7 million tonnes of tuna are harvested annually, making it vital for global food security. Tuna species, especially Bluefin tuna in the Northern Pacific, are threatened by overfishing. Around 51.7% of global tuna catch is currently certified as sustainable. The day emphasises responsible fishing practices and long-term conservation of marine resources.

questions.question: Mahua flower festival (Ippa Puvvu Panduga) is mainly celebrated in which state?
questions.explanation: The annual Mahua flower festival, known as ‘Ippa Puvvu Panduga’, is celebrated in Telangana. The festival takes place in Jamidi village of Narnoor mandal in Adilabad district. It is usually observed during April–May, coinciding with the Mahua flower harvest season. The festival highlights the cultural and economic importance of the Mahua tree for tribal communities. It is mainly celebrated by the Gond and other Adivasi communities. Mahua flowers are used for food products, traditional beverages, and medicinal purposes. The event is organised by tribal organisations and local leaders.

questions.question: Which state has launched India’s first State-led Centre of Excellence for Space Technology?
questions.explanation: Karnataka has launched India’s first State-led Centre of Excellence for Space Technology (CoE SpaceTech Foundation) in Bengaluru. The initiative aims to convert space innovation into scalable and commercial outcomes. The Centre has been established by the Karnataka Innovation and Technology Society in collaboration with the Satcom Industry Association of India. It focuses on advanced research, industry-oriented training, and talent development in space technologies. The Centre will act as a hub for startup incubation and ecosystem building. It promotes collaboration between government, industry, and academia.

questions.question: International Scurvy Awareness Day is observed annually on which day?
questions.explanation: International Scurvy Awareness Day is observed annually on May 2 to spread awareness about Vitamin C deficiency. Scurvy is caused by a lack of Vitamin C (ascorbic acid) in the diet. The day highlights that scurvy is not just a historical disease but still occurs today due to poor nutrition. It mainly affects people with limited or unbalanced diets, including the elderly and picky eaters. The disease can be prevented by consuming Vitamin C-rich foods like citrus fruits, guava, tomatoes, and green vegetables. Scurvy is fully preventable and treatable through proper diet and supplementation. The day promotes better nutrition and awareness for overall public health.
